---
title: "Replication Paper Coding"
author: "Xolani Ncala"
date: "2025-10-31"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, fig.width = 14, fig.height = 10, out.width = "100%")
```


```{r, libsload, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# 1. Core tidyverse tools (load first to establish base verbs)
library(tidyverse)  # Includes ggplot2, dplyr, tidyr, readr, purrr, tibble, stringr

# 2. Data import and harmonization
library(haven)       # For .sav files
library(countrycode) # For country code harmonization

# 3. Modeling and statistical tools
library(MASS)        # For polr (ordinal regression)
library(nnet)        # For multinomial logistic regression
library(car)         # For recoding, VIF, etc.
library(vcd)         # For categorical data visualization

# 4. Table formatting and reporting
library(broom)       # For tidying model outputs
library(knitr)       # For markdown tables
library(kableExtra)  # For enhanced table styling


# 5. Visualization (already loaded via tidyverse, but reloading if needed)
library(ggplot2)


##SOME PACKAKEGES ARE RELOADED IN SUCCESSSIVE CHUNKS TO AVOID PACKAGE CONCFLICT##

```


```{r, dtload}

#Loading the .sav file into data frame 
Afrobaro.Roun6.Data <- read_sav("Afr.roun6.data.36coun.sav", encoding = "latin1") 

# Loading the .csv file into data frame
GDP <- read.csv("GDP.PPP.csv", skip = 3, sep = ",")
```

### This is a replication for table 1. 

```{r, dt1load}

#Loading the .sav file into data frame 
Afrobaro.Roun6.Data <- read_sav("Afr.roun6.data.36coun.sav", encoding = "latin1") 

# Loading the .csv file into data frame
GDP <- read.csv("GDP.PPP.csv", skip = 3, sep = ",")
```

```{r, Table1, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Step 1: Convert labelled COUNTRY to character and move to front
Afrobaro.Roun6.Data <- Afrobaro.Roun6.Data |>
  mutate(COUNTRY_NAME = as.character(as_factor(COUNTRY))) |>
  relocate(COUNTRY_NAME)

# This is fixing the Sao Tome Principe country name 
Afrobaro.Roun6.Data <- Afrobaro.Roun6.Data |>
  mutate(COUNTRY_NAME = str_replace(COUNTRY_NAME, "S.*Tom.*Pr.*ncipe", "Sao Tome and Principe"))


# Step 2: Define and clean country list to select our 32 African countries 
selected_countries <- c(
  "Cape Verde", "Mauritius", "South Africa", "Botswana", "Namibia", "Gabon", "Zambia",
  "Ghana", "Nigeria", "Swaziland", "Senegal", "Tanzania", "Guinea", "Cote d'Ivoire",
  "Zimbabwe", "Kenya", "Cameroon", "Benin", "Lesotho", "Mali", "Sudan", "Burkina Faso",
  "Togo", "Sierra Leone", "Uganda", "Madagascar", "Niger", "Mozambique", "Malawi",
  "Sao Tome and Principe", "Liberia", "Burundi"
)

Afr_roun6_subset32 <- Afrobaro.Roun6.Data |>
  filter(COUNTRY_NAME %in% selected_countries)



# Step 3:  We attach ISO3 codes to the Afrobarometer Data and then we count sample sizes 
country_codes <- tibble(
  COUNTRY_NAME = selected_countries,
  ISO3 = countrycode(selected_countries, origin = "country.name", destination = "iso3c")
)

country_counts <- Afr_roun6_subset32 |>
  count(COUNTRY_NAME, name = "n") |>
  left_join(country_codes, by = "COUNTRY_NAME") |>
  arrange(desc(n)) 




# Step 4: Merge GDP into country_codes
GDP <- GDP |>
  mutate(ISO3 = countrycode(Country.Name, origin = "country.name", destination = "iso3c"))

country_codes_gdp <- country_codes |>
  left_join(GDP, by = "ISO3")

# then we Attach ISO3 to Afrobarometer subset
Afr_roun6_subset32 <- Afr_roun6_subset32 |>
  left_join(country_codes, by = "COUNTRY_NAME")

# then we Merge GDP into Afrobarometer subset using ISO3
Afr_roun6_with_GDP <- Afr_roun6_subset32 |>
  left_join(dplyr::select(GDP, ISO3, Country.Name, X2018), by = "ISO3")




# Step 5: The Grouping of the contries under GDP PPP

AFB.ROUND.6 <- Afr_roun6_with_GDP |>
  dplyr::select(
    COUNTRY_NAME, ISO3, X2018, RESPNO, URBRUR, Q101, Q1, Q97, Q95,
    Q8E, EA_SVC_A, Q8B, Q8C, Q91A, Q91B, Q91C, Q91D
  ) |>
  dplyr::rename(
    country = COUNTRY_NAME,
    code = ISO3,
    gdp.ppp = X2018,
    resp.no = RESPNO,
    res.area = URBRUR,
    sex = Q101,
    Age = Q1,
    education = Q97,
    employment.status = Q95,
    income.available = Q8E,
    electricity = EA_SVC_A,
    water.insecurity = Q8B,
    food.insecurity = Q8C,
    radio = Q91A,
    tv = Q91B,
    car.or.bike = Q91C,
    phone = Q91D
  )


#  Step 6: Creating the GDP.PPP groups 

AFB.ROUND.6<- AFB.ROUND.6 |>
  mutate(GDP_PPP_Group = case_when(
    gdp.ppp>= 4000 ~ "GDP PPP (\\$4,000 and more )",
    gdp.ppp >= 2000 ~ "GDP PPP (\\$2,000 to \\$3,999)",
    gdp.ppp >= 1500 ~ "GDP PPP (\\$1,500 to \\$1,999)",
    gdp.ppp< 1500 ~ "GDP PPP (Less than \\$1,500)",
    TRUE ~ "Unknown"
  ))


# Step 7: Summarize sample sizes by country and gdp.ppp bracket
table1 <- AFB.ROUND.6|>
  count(GDP_PPP_Group, country, gdp.ppp, name = "Sample_Size") |>
  arrange(GDP_PPP_Group, desc(Sample_Size))



#  Step 8: We Prepare the table

# Step 1: Sort by descending GDP PPP
table1_sorted <- table1 |>
  arrange(desc(gdp.ppp)) |>
  dplyr::select(
    Country = country,
    `GDP PPP (2018) \\$` = gdp.ppp,
    `Sample Size` = Sample_Size,
    Group = GDP_PPP_Group
  )


# Step 2: Render with grouped rows
table1_sorted |>
  kable(format = "html", caption = "Table 1. Sample size of studied countries in SSA (n = 49,137)", align = "lccr") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 4, valign = "top")

```




```{r, Table1_TeX, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}


library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(stringr)
# Make sure your GDP_PPP_Group variable has proper labels
AFB.ROUND.6 <- AFB.ROUND.6 |>
  mutate(GDP_PPP_Group = case_when(
    gdp.ppp >= 4000 ~ "GDP PPP ($4,000 and more)",
    gdp.ppp >= 2000 & gdp.ppp < 4000 ~ "GDP PPP ($2,000 to $3,999)",
    gdp.ppp >= 1500 & gdp.ppp < 2000 ~ "GDP PPP ($1,500 to $1,999)",
    TRUE ~ "GDP PPP (Less than $1,500)"
  ))

# Summarize and order
table1_tex <- AFB.ROUND.6 |>
  count(GDP_PPP_Group, country, gdp.ppp, name = "Sample_Size") |>
  mutate(GDP_PPP_Group = factor(
    GDP_PPP_Group,
    levels = c(
      "GDP PPP ($4,000 and more)",
      "GDP PPP ($2,000 to $3,999)",
      "GDP PPP ($1,500 to $1,999)",
      "GDP PPP (Less than $1,500)"
    )
  )) |>
  arrange(GDP_PPP_Group, desc(gdp.ppp)) |>
  dplyr::select(
    `GDP PPP Group` = GDP_PPP_Group,
    Country = country,
    `GDP PPP (2018, $)` = gdp.ppp,
    `Sample Size` = Sample_Size
  )



# Generate clean LaTeX table
latex_table <- table1_tex |>
  kable(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = "llcc",
    caption = "Sample size of studied countries in sub-Saharan Africa (n = 49,137)"
  ) |>
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position")) |>
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "middle")

# Save LaTeX code to a file
cat(latex_table, file = "Table1_SSA_SampleSize_Clean.tex")

```


### This is replication for table 2

```{r, tab2, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

#This section we are recoding all the variables to be how Ahmadi used them 

# step1 start with res.area recoding to urban and rural only 

AFB.ROUND.6 <- AFB.ROUND.6 |>
  mutate(
    res.area = case_when(
      res.area == 1 ~ 0,
      res.area == 2 ~ 1,
      res.area == 3 ~ 0,
      res.area == 460 ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    res.area = factor(res.area, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Urban", "Rural")),
                            #recoding gender/sex
    sex = case_when(
      sex == -1 ~ NA_real_,      
      sex == 1 ~ 0,
      sex == 2 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    sex = factor(sex, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Male", "Female")),
                            #recoding Age 
    Age = case_when(
      Age %in% c(-1, 98, 999) ~ NA_real_,
      Age >= 18 & Age <= 25 ~ 0,
      Age >= 26 & Age <= 35 ~ 1,
      Age >= 36 & Age <= 45 ~ 2,
      Age >= 46 & Age <= 55 ~ 3,
      Age >= 56 & Age <= 65 ~ 4,
      Age >= 66 ~ 5,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    Age = factor(Age, levels = 0:5,
                 labels = c("18–25", "26–35", "36–45", "46–55", "56–65", "66 and over")),
                                      #recoding education
    education = case_when(
      education %in% c(-1, 98, 99) ~ NA_real_,
      education %in% c(0, 1) ~ 0,
      education %in% c(2, 3) ~ 1,
      education %in% c(4, 5) ~ 2,
      education %in% c(6, 7, 8, 9) ~ 3,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    education = factor(education, levels = 0:3,
                       labels = c("No formal education", "Primary", "Secondary", "Post-secondary")),
                                          #rec emplyment
    employment.status = case_when(
      employment.status %in% c(-1, 9, 98) ~ NA_real_,
      employment.status %in% c(0, 1) ~ 0,
      employment.status == 2 ~ 1,
      employment.status == 3 ~ 2,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    employment.status = factor(employment.status,
                               levels = 0:2,
                               labels = c("Unemployed", "Employed part-time", "Employed full-time")),
                                    #rec Income
    income.available = case_when(
      income.available %in% c(-1, 9, 98) ~ NA_real_,
      income.available == 0 ~ 0,
      income.available == 1 ~ 1,
      income.available == 2 ~ 2,
      income.available == 3 ~ 3,
      income.available == 4 ~ 4,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    income.available = factor(income.available, levels = 0:4,
                              labels = c("Never", "Just once or twice", "Several times", "Many times", "Always")),
                                  #recoding electricity
    electricity = case_when(
      electricity %in% c(-1, 9) ~ NA_real_,
      electricity == 0 ~ 0,
      electricity == 1 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    electricity = factor(electricity, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes")),
                                #recoding water 
    water.insecurity = case_when(
      water.insecurity %in% c(-1, 9, 98) ~ NA_real_,
      water.insecurity == 0 ~ 0,
      water.insecurity == 1 ~ 1,
      water.insecurity == 2 ~ 2,
      water.insecurity == 3 ~ 3,
      water.insecurity == 4 ~ 4,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    water.insecurity = factor(water.insecurity, levels = 0:4,
                              labels = c("Never", "Just once or twice", "Several times", "Many times", "Always")),
                                #recoding food 
    food.insecurity = case_when(
      food.insecurity %in% c(-1, 9, 98) ~ NA_real_,
      food.insecurity == 0 ~ 0,
      food.insecurity == 1 ~ 1,
      food.insecurity == 2 ~ 2,
      food.insecurity == 3 ~ 3,
      food.insecurity == 4 ~ 4,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    food.insecurity = factor(food.insecurity, levels = 0:4,
                             labels = c("Never", "Just once or twice", "Several times", "Many times", "Always")))
    
  AFB.ROUND.6 <- AFB.ROUND.6 |>
  mutate(
    radio = ifelse(radio %in% c(-1, 9, 98), NA_real_, radio),
    tv = ifelse(tv %in% c(-1, 9, 98), NA_real_, tv),
    car.or.bike = ifelse(car.or.bike %in% c(-1, 9, 98), NA_real_, car.or.bike),
    phone = ifelse(phone %in% c(-1, 9, 98), NA_real_, phone)
  )
                              #recoding asset score
AFB.ROUND.6 <- AFB.ROUND.6 |>
  mutate(
    asset_ownership_raw = rowSums(dplyr::select(AFB.ROUND.6, radio, tv, car.or.bike, phone), na.rm = TRUE),
    asset_ownership = case_when(
      asset_ownership_raw == 4 ~ "Owns all 4",
      asset_ownership_raw == 3 ~ "Owns 3",
      asset_ownership_raw == 2 ~ "Owns 2",
      asset_ownership_raw == 1 ~ "Owns 1",
      asset_ownership_raw == 0 ~ "Owns none",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    asset_ownership = factor(asset_ownership,
                             levels = c("Owns all 4", "Owns 3", "Owns 2", "Owns 1", "Owns none"))
  )



# Variables to summarize
vars <- c("res.area", "sex", "Age", "education", "employment.status",
          "income.available", "electricity", "water.insecurity",
          "food.insecurity", "asset_ownership")

# Summarize function
summarize_variable <- function(df, var) {
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[var]])) %>%
    count(!!sym(var), name = "Frequency") %>%
    mutate(
      Percent = round(100 * Frequency / sum(Frequency), 1),
      Variable = var,
      Category = as.character(!!sym(var))
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(Variable, Category, Frequency, Percent)
}

# Build table
table2 <- bind_rows(lapply(vars, function(v) summarize_variable(AFB.ROUND.6, v)))

# Make variable names readable
table2 <- table2 %>%
  mutate(Variable = case_when(
    Variable == "res.area" ~ "Area of residence",
    Variable == "sex" ~ "Sex",
    Variable == "Age" ~ "Age group",
    Variable == "education" ~ "Education",
    Variable == "employment.status" ~ "Employment",
    Variable == "income.available" ~ "Income availability",
    Variable == "electricity" ~ "Electricity access",
    Variable == "water.insecurity" ~ "Water insecurity",
    Variable == "food.insecurity" ~ "Food insecurity",
    Variable == "asset_ownership" ~ "Asset ownership",
    TRUE ~ Variable
  ))

# Create LaTeX table with multirow for variables
latex_table2 <- table2 %>%
  kable(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    caption = "Characteristics of respondents (n = 49,137)",
    col.names = c("Variable", "Category", "Frequency", "Percent"),
    align = c("l", "l", "c", "c"),
    linesep = ""
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position")) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") %>%  # Collapse Variable names
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)                        # Bold header row

# Wrap the whole table in \small
latex_table2 <- paste0("\\small\n", latex_table2)

# Save LaTeX table
cat(latex_table2, file = "Table2_SSA_Characteristics2.tex")


print(table2, row.names = TRUE)

```

### Now recreating Figure 1 of the article 

```{r, fig1, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

Afr_roun6_with_GDP <- Afr_roun6_with_GDP %>%
  mutate(
    food.insecurity = case_when(
      Q8A %in% c(0:4) ~ as.character(Q8A),
      Q8A %in% c(-1, 9, 98) ~ NA_character_
    ),
    water.insecurity = case_when(
      Q8B %in% c(0:4) ~ as.character(Q8B),
      Q8B %in% c(-1, 9, 98) ~ NA_character_
    ),
    # Label the levels for readability
    food.insecurity = factor(food.insecurity,
                             levels = c("0", "1", "2", "3", "4"),
                             labels = c("Never", "Just once or twice", "Several times", "Many times", "Always")),
    water.insecurity = factor(water.insecurity,
                              levels = c("0", "1", "2", "3", "4"),
                              labels = c("Never", "Just once or twice", "Several times", "Many times", "Always"))
  )


# Get list of countries
countries <- unique(Afr_roun6_with_GDP$COUNTRY_NAME)

# Initialize empty dataframe for results
country_secure <- data.frame()

# Loop through each country
for (c in countries) {
  
  # Subset country
  subset_data <- Afr_roun6_with_GDP %>% filter(COUNTRY_NAME == c)
  
  # Remove NAs within this country
  food_valid <- subset_data %>% filter(!is.na(food.insecurity))
  water_valid <- subset_data %>% filter(!is.na(water.insecurity))
  
  # Calculate percentages of "Never"
  food_pct <- mean(food_valid$food.insecurity == "Never") * 100
  water_pct <- mean(water_valid$water.insecurity == "Never") * 100
  
  # Combine into dataframe
  country_secure <- rbind(country_secure,
                          data.frame(COUNTRY_NAME = c,
                                     food_secure = food_pct,
                                     water_secure = water_pct))
}

# Convert to long format for plotting
country_secure_long <- country_secure %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(food_secure, water_secure),
    names_to = "Type",
    values_to = "Percent"
  )

# Use the per-country dataset we created
fig1_plot <- ggplot(country_secure_long, aes(x = reorder(COUNTRY_NAME, Percent), y = Percent, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", Percent)),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3.2) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = "Country",
    y = "Percentage (%)",
    fill = ""
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("food_secure" = "lightgreen", "water_secure" = "darkblue"),
                    labels = c("Food secure", "Water secure")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 105)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.margin = margin(t = 20, r = 20, b = 20, l = 20)
  )

ggsave("Figure1_Food_Water_Security.pdf", plot = fig1_plot, width = 12, height = 8, units = "in")

fig1_plot
```

### Now replicating the bivariate statistics table


```{r, tab3, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

#1. Recoding variables 

Afr_roun6_with_GDP <- Afr_roun6_with_GDP %>%
  mutate(
    # 
    # Food & water insecurity
    # 
    food.insecurity = case_when(Q8A %in% 0:4 ~ as.character(Q8A), TRUE ~ NA_character_),
    water.insecurity = case_when(Q8B %in% 0:4 ~ as.character(Q8B), TRUE ~ NA_character_),
    food.insecurity = factor(food.insecurity,
                             levels = c("0", "1", "2", "3", "4"),
                             labels = c("Never", "Just once or twice", "Several times", "Many times", "Always")),
    water.insecurity = factor(water.insecurity,
                              levels = c("0", "1", "2", "3", "4"),
                              labels = c("Never", "Just once or twice", "Several times", "Many times", "Always")),

    # 
    # Gender
    # 
    gender = factor(case_when(
      Q101 == 1 ~ 0,
      Q101 == 2 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    levels = 0:1,
    labels = c("Male", "Female")),

    # 
    # Urban / Rural
    #
    urban_rural = factor(case_when(
      URBRUR == 1 ~ 0,
      URBRUR == 2 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    levels = 0:1,
    labels = c("Urban", "Rural")),

    # 
    # Employment status
    # 
    employment = factor(case_when(
      Q95 %in% c(-1, 9, 98) ~ NA_real_,
      Q95 == 2 ~ 1,
      Q95 %in% c(0, 1) ~ 0,
      Q95 == 3 ~ 2,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    levels = 0:2,
    labels = c("Unemployed", "Employed part-time", "Employed full-time")),

    # 
    # Education level
    # 
    education = factor(case_when(
      Q97 %in% c(-1, 98, 99) ~ NA_real_,
      Q97 %in% c(0, 1) ~ 0,
      Q97 %in% c(2, 3) ~ 1,
      Q97 %in% c(4, 5) ~ 2,
      Q97 %in% c(6, 7, 8, 9) ~ 3,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    levels = 0:3,
    labels = c("No formal education", "Primary", "Secondary", "Post-secondary")),

    # 
    # Age group
    # 
    age_group = factor(case_when(
      Q1 >= 18 & Q1 <= 25 ~ 0,
      Q1 >= 26 & Q1 <= 35 ~ 1,
      Q1 >= 36 & Q1 <= 45 ~ 2,
      Q1 >= 46 & Q1 <= 55 ~ 3,
      Q1 >= 56 & Q1 <= 65 ~ 4,
      Q1 >= 66 ~ 5,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    levels = 0:5,
    labels = c("18–25", "26–35", "36–45", "46–55", "56–65", "66 and over")),

    # 
    # Income availability
    # 
    income_available = factor(case_when(
      Q8E %in% 0:4 ~ Q8E,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    levels = 0:4,
    labels = c("Never", "Just once or twice", "Several times", "Many times", "Always")),

    # 
    # Electricity access
    # 
    electricity = factor(case_when(
      EA_SVC_A %in% c(-1, 9) ~ NA_real_,
      EA_SVC_A == 0 ~ 0,
      EA_SVC_A == 1 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    levels = 0:1,
    labels = c("No electricity", "Has electricity"))
  )

# 
# Individual asset indicators
# 
Afr_roun6_with_GDP <- Afr_roun6_with_GDP %>%
  mutate(
    radio = case_when(Q91A %in% 0:1 ~ Q91A,
                      Q91A %in% c(-1, 9, 98) ~ NA_real_),
    tv = case_when(Q91B %in% 0:1 ~ Q91B,
                   Q91B %in% c(-1, 9, 98) ~ NA_real_),
    car_or_bike = case_when(Q91C %in% 0:1 ~ Q91C,
                            Q91C %in% c(-1, 9, 98) ~ NA_real_),
    phone = case_when(Q91D %in% 0:1 ~ Q91D,
                      Q91D %in% c(-1, 9, 98) ~ NA_real_)
  )

# 
# Asset ownership summary
# 
Afr_roun6_with_GDP <- Afr_roun6_with_GDP %>%
  mutate(
    asset_ownership_raw = rowSums(dplyr::select(., radio, tv, car_or_bike, phone), na.rm = TRUE),
    asset_ownership = factor(case_when(
      asset_ownership_raw == 0 ~ "Owns none",
      asset_ownership_raw == 1 ~ "Owns 1",
      asset_ownership_raw == 2 ~ "Owns 2",
      asset_ownership_raw == 3 ~ "Owns 3",
      asset_ownership_raw == 4 ~ "Owns all 4",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    levels = c("Owns none", "Owns 1", "Owns 2", "Owns 3", "Owns all 4"))
  )


# 
# 2 Computing Spearman correlations
# 

compute_spearman <- function(var1, var2, df){
  df_sub <- df %>% dplyr::select({{var1}}, all_of(var2)) %>% drop_na()
  cor.test(as.numeric(df_sub[[1]]),
           as.numeric(df_sub[[2]]),
           method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
}

# List of variables to correlate with food insecurity
vars_to_test <- c("water.insecurity", "gender", "age_group", "urban_rural",
                  "education", "employment", "income_available", "electricity", "asset_ownership")

# Corresponding labels for display
labels <- c("Water insecurity", "Gender", "Age group", "Area of residence",
            "Education", "Employment", "Income availability", "Electricity", "Asset ownership")

# Compute results safely and track valid variables through loop
valid_results <- list()
valid_labels <- c()

for (i in seq_along(vars_to_test)) {
  var <- vars_to_test[i]
  label <- labels[i]
  if (var %in% names(Afr_roun6_with_GDP)) {
    result <- tryCatch({
      compute_spearman(food.insecurity, var, Afr_roun6_with_GDP)
    }, error = function(e) NULL)
    if (!is.null(result)) {
      valid_results[[length(valid_results) + 1]] <- result
      valid_labels <- c(valid_labels, label)
    }
  } else {
    warning(paste("Variable not found:", var))
  }
}

# 
# 3 Combine results into a table
# 

table3 <- tibble(
  Variable = valid_labels,
  Strength_Spearman_rho = round(sapply(valid_results, function(x) x$estimate), 3),
  Sig_level = format.pval(sapply(valid_results, function(x) x$p.value), digits = 3, eps = 0.001)
)

#
# 4 Export to LaTeX (.tex)
# 

library(kableExtra)

table3_tex <- table3 %>%
  kable(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    caption = "Table 3. Independent Spearman’s rho correlations between food insecurity and socio-economic factors in SSA countries (n = 49,137).",
    col.names = c("Variable", "Strength (Spearman’s $\\rho$)", "Sig. level"),
    align = "lcc",
    digits = 3,
    escape = FALSE
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position", "striped", "scale_down"))

# Save LaTeX table
writeLines(table3_tex, "table3_spearman.tex")

table3


```



# Replicating the Unadjusted Multinomial model

```{r, multinom1, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

Afr_roun6_with_GDP <- Afr_roun6_with_GDP %>%
  mutate(
    food.insecurity   = relevel(food.insecurity, ref = "Always"),
    water.insecurity  = relevel(water.insecurity, ref = "Always"),
    gender            = relevel(gender, ref = "Male"),
    age_group         = relevel(age_group, ref = "66 and over"),
    urban_rural       = relevel(urban_rural, ref = "Urban"),
    employment        = relevel(employment, ref = "Employed part-time"),
    income_available  = relevel(income_available, ref = "Never"), 
    electricity       =  relevel(electricity, ref = "Has electricity"),
    asset_ownership   = relevel(asset_ownership, ref = "Owns all 4"),
    education         = relevel(education, ref = "Post-secondary")
  )

# 
# 2 Run unadjusted multinomial models
#
model.water       <- multinom(food.insecurity ~ water.insecurity, data = Afr_roun6_with_GDP)
model.gender      <- multinom(food.insecurity ~ gender, data = Afr_roun6_with_GDP)
model.age         <- multinom(food.insecurity ~ age_group, data = Afr_roun6_with_GDP)
model.area        <- multinom(food.insecurity ~ urban_rural, data = Afr_roun6_with_GDP)
model.employ      <- multinom(food.insecurity ~ employment, data = Afr_roun6_with_GDP)
model.income      <- multinom(food.insecurity ~ income_available, data = Afr_roun6_with_GDP)
model.asset       <- multinom(food.insecurity ~ asset_ownership, data = Afr_roun6_with_GDP)
model.education   <- multinom(food.insecurity ~ education, data = Afr_roun6_with_GDP)
model.electricity <- multinom(food.insecurity ~ electricity, data = Afr_roun6_with_GDP)

# 
# 3 Define safe extract_table() function
# 
extract_table <- function(model, varname) {
  df <- tidy(model, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(
      or_ci = paste0(
        round(estimate, 3), " (",
        round(conf.low, 3), "–",
        round(conf.high, 3), ")"
      )
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(y.level, term, or_ci) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = y.level, values_from = or_ci) %>%
    rename(Category = term) %>%
    mutate(Variable = varname)

  # Ensure all outcome columns exist
  expected_cols <- c("Never", "Just once or twice", "Several times", "Many times")
  for (col in expected_cols) {
    if (!col %in% names(df)) df[[col]] <- NA_character_
  }

  df %>%
    dplyr::select(Variable, Category, all_of(expected_cols))
}




# 
# 4. Combine all model outputs
# 
table4_df <- bind_rows(
  extract_table(model.water,       "Water insecurity"),
  extract_table(model.gender,      "Gender"),
  extract_table(model.age,         "Age group"),
  extract_table(model.area,        "Area of residence"),
  extract_table(model.employ,      "Employment"),
  extract_table(model.income,      "Income availability"),
  extract_table(model.asset,       "Asset ownership"),
  extract_table(model.education,   "Education"),
  extract_table(model.electricity, "Electricity access")
)



# 
# Clean up Category labels (Electricity fix included)
# 
table4_df <- table4_df %>%
  mutate(
    Category = case_when(
      grepl("water\\.insecurity", Category)     ~ str_remove(Category, "water\\.insecurity"),
      grepl("gender", Category)                 ~ str_remove(Category, "gender"),
      grepl("age_group", Category)              ~ str_remove(Category, "age_group"),
      grepl("urban_rural", Category)            ~ str_remove(Category, "urban_rural"),
      grepl("employment", Category)             ~ str_remove(Category, "employment"),
      grepl("income_available", Category)       ~ str_remove(Category, "income_available"),
      grepl("asset_ownership", Category)        ~ str_remove(Category, "asset_ownership"),
      grepl("education", Category)              ~ str_remove(Category, "education"),
      grepl("electricity", Category)            ~ str_remove(Category, "electricity"),
      TRUE                                     ~ Category
    ),
    # Replace underscores and dots with spaces
    Category = gsub("_", " ", Category),
    Category = gsub("\\.", " ", Category),
    Category = gsub("\\s+", " ", Category),
    Category = str_trim(Category),
    # Capitalize words properly
    Category = str_to_title(Category)
  )



# 
# 5️ Generate LaTeX Table (grouped and formatted)
# 
table4_tex <- table4_df %>%
  kbl(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    escape = FALSE,
    align = c("l", "l", "c", "c", "c", "c"),
    caption = "Table 4. Unadjusted multinomial logistic regression analyses of Food Insecurity by socio-economic factors in SSA countries (n = 49,137)"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("HOLD_position", "striped", "scale_down"),
    full_width = FALSE,
    font_size = 9
  ) %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE, width = "4cm") %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top") %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 2, "Odds Ratios (95% CI)" = 4)) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 2, "Food Insecurity Levels" = 4)) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)





# 
# Save LaTeX output to .tex file
# 
writeLines(table4_tex, "Table4_Unadjusted_Multinomial2.tex")
table4_df
```

#Now replicating adjusted 

```{r,adjusted, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE} 
# 
# 1️ Fit Adjusted Multinomial Model
# 
model.adjusted <- multinom(
  food.insecurity ~ water.insecurity + gender + age_group + urban_rural + employment + electricity,
  data = Afr_roun6_with_GDP,
  trace = TRUE,
  model = TRUE
)

# 
# 2️ Safe extraction of ORs and CIs including Electricity
#
extract_table_safe <- function(model, model_label = "Adjusted model (controls + electricity)") {
  tidy(model, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(
      Variable = case_when(
        grepl("water", term, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Water Insecurity",
        grepl("gender", term, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Gender",
        grepl("age_group", term, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Age Group",
        grepl("urban_rural", term, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Urban/Rural",
        grepl("employment", term, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Employment",
        grepl("electricity", term, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Electricity Access",
        TRUE ~ term
      ),
      Category = term %>%
        str_remove(".*?\\.") %>%
        str_replace_all("_", " ") %>%
        str_replace_all("–", "--") %>%
        str_to_title() %>%
        str_trim(),
      OR_CI = sprintf("%.3f (%.3f--%.3f)", estimate, conf.low, conf.high)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(y.level, Variable, Category, OR_CI) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = y.level, values_from = OR_CI) %>%
    relocate(Variable, Category)
}

table5_safe <- extract_table_safe(model.adjusted)

# 
# 3️ Model fit statistics (for reference line)
# 

null_model <- multinom(food.insecurity ~ 1, data = Afr_roun6_with_GDP)

ll_full <- as.numeric(logLik(model.adjusted))
ll_null <- as.numeric(logLik(null_model))
minus2LL <- -2 * ll_full
chi_sq <- -2 * (ll_null - ll_full)

df_diff <- attr(logLik(model.adjusted), "df") - attr(logLik(null_model), "df")
p_value <- pchisq(chi_sq, df = df_diff, lower.tail = FALSE)
pseudoR2 <- DescTools::PseudoR2(model.adjusted, which = c("CoxSnell", "Nagelkerke", "McFadden"))
AIC_val <- AIC(model.adjusted)
BIC_val <- BIC(model.adjusted)

# 
# 4 Generate LaTeX Table (main table only)
#
table5_tex_main <- table5_safe %>%
  kbl(
    "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    escape = FALSE,
    align = "lcccccc",
    caption = "Table 5. Adjusted multinomial logistic regression analyses between water insecurity and food insecurity by controlling for gender, age, area of residence, employment, and electricity in sub-Saharan Africa (n = 49,137)"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("striped", "hold_position", "scale_down")
  ) %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1:2, valign = "top")

#
# 5. Create Reference Line (model statistics)
# 
model_stats_line <- sprintf(
  "\\textit{Reference:} -2 Log Likelihood (Full Model): %.3f; Likelihood Ratio Chi-square: %.3f, df = %d, p = %.4g; Pseudo R-squared (CoxSnell / Nagelkerke / McFadden): %.3f / %.3f / %.3f; AIC = %.3f; BIC = %.3f",
  minus2LL, chi_sq, df_diff, p_value,
  pseudoR2["CoxSnell"], pseudoR2["Nagelkerke"], pseudoR2["McFadden"],
  AIC_val, BIC_val
)

# 
# 6 Export to .tex file
#
cat(
  table5_tex_main,
  "\n", model_stats_line,
  "\n\\end{table}",
  file = "Table5_Adjusted_Multinomial_withElectricity_AICBIC.tex"
)
table5_safe
model_stats_line
```




## I am suggesting that we should actualy run an ordinal logistic regression  


```{r, mineh, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Create a new data frame with the original (uncleaned) variables
 

# 1. Data Preparation
#
library(dplyr)

ord_reg_data <- Afr_roun6_with_GDP[, c(
  "ISO3", "URBRUR", "Q101", "Q1", "Q97", "Q95", "X2018",
  "Q8A", "Q8B", "Q8E", "asset_ownership", "EA_SVC_A"
)]

colnames(ord_reg_data) <- c(
  "Countrycode", "Area_of_Residence", "Gender", "Age",
  "Education", "Employment", "GDP_PPP",
  "Food_Insecurity", "Water_Insecurity",
  "Income_Insecurity", "Asset_Ownership", "Electricity_access"
)

ord_reg_data <- ord_reg_data %>%
  mutate(
    # Area of Residence
    Area_of_Residence = case_when(
      Area_of_Residence %in% c(1, 3, 460) ~ 0,
      Area_of_Residence == 2 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    Area_of_Residence = factor(Area_of_Residence, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Urban", "Rural")),

    # Gender
    Gender = case_when(
      Gender == 1 ~ 0,
      Gender == 2 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    Gender = factor(Gender, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Male", "Female")),

    # Age
    Age = case_when(
      Age %in% c(-1, 998, 999) ~ NA_real_,
      Age >= 18 & Age <= 105 ~ as.numeric(Age),
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),

    # Education
    Education = case_when(
      Education %in% c(-1, 98, 99) ~ NA_real_,
      Education %in% 0:9 ~ Education + 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),

    # Employment
    Employment = case_when(
      Employment %in% c(-1, 9, 98) ~ NA_real_,
      Employment == 2 ~ 2,
      Employment %in% c(0, 1) ~ 1,
      Employment == 3 ~ 3,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    Employment = factor(Employment, levels = 1:3,
                        labels = c("Unemployed", "Employed part-time", "Employed full-time")),
    Employment = relevel(Employment, ref = "Employed full-time"),

    # Electricity access (ref = "No electricity")
    Electricity_access = case_when(
      Electricity_access %in% c(-1, 9) ~ NA_real_,
      Electricity_access == 0 ~ 0,
      Electricity_access == 1 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    Electricity_access = factor(Electricity_access,
                                levels = c(0, 1),
                                labels = c("No electricity", "Has electricity")),
    Electricity_access = relevel(Electricity_access, ref = "Has electricity"),

    # Food Insecurity (ordinal outcome)
    Food_Insecurity = case_when(
      Food_Insecurity %in% c(-1, 9, 98) ~ NA_real_,
      Food_Insecurity %in% 0:4 ~ Food_Insecurity + 1,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    Food_Insecurity = factor(Food_Insecurity, levels = 1:5,
                             labels = c("Never", "Once or twice", "Several times", "Many times", "Always"),
                             ordered = TRUE),

    # Water, Income, and Assets
    Water_Insecurity = ifelse(Water_Insecurity %in% c(-1, 9, 98), NA, Water_Insecurity + 1),
    Income_Insecurity = ifelse(Income_Insecurity %in% c(-1, 9, 98), NA, Income_Insecurity + 1),
    Asset_Ownership = case_when(
      Asset_Ownership == "Owns none"  ~ 0,
      Asset_Ownership == "Owns 1"     ~ 1,
      Asset_Ownership == "Owns 2"     ~ 2,
      Asset_Ownership == "Owns 3"     ~ 3,
      Asset_Ownership == "Owns all 4" ~ 4,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    GDP_PPP = as.numeric(GDP_PPP),

    # GDP group
    GDP_PPP_Group = case_when(
      GDP_PPP < 1000 ~ 1,
      GDP_PPP < 2000 ~ 2,
      GDP_PPP < 3000 ~ 3,
      GDP_PPP < 4000 ~ 4,
      GDP_PPP >= 4000 ~ 5,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    GDP_PPP_Group = factor(GDP_PPP_Group, levels = 1:5,
                           labels = c("GDP PPP (< $1,000)",
                                      "GDP PPP ($1,000–$1,999)",
                                      "GDP PPP ($2,000–$2,999)",
                                      "GDP PPP ($3,000–$3,999)",
                                      "GDP PPP ($4,000 and more)")),
    GDP_PPP_Group = relevel(GDP_PPP_Group, ref = "GDP PPP ($4,000 and more)")
  )


# 
# 2. Ordinal Model
#
model_ordinal <- polr(
  Food_Insecurity ~ Water_Insecurity + Gender + Area_of_Residence + Age +
    Employment + Income_Insecurity + Education + Asset_Ownership + Electricity_access + GDP_PPP_Group,
  data = ord_reg_data,
  Hess = TRUE
)




# 
# 3 Extract Coefficients, OR, and 95% CIs
# 
coefs <- coef(summary(model_ordinal))
coefs_predictors <- coefs[!grepl("\\|", rownames(coefs)), , drop = FALSE]
ci <- confint(model_ordinal)
OR <- exp(coef(model_ordinal))
ci_OR <- exp(ci)  # to our match predictor names only

#  Clean variable names for readability

clean_var_names <- names(OR)

clean_var_names <- clean_var_names %>%
  gsub("Water_Insecurity", "Water insecurity", .) %>%
  gsub("GenderFemale", "Female (vs Male)", .) %>%
  gsub("Area_of_ResidenceRural", "Rural (vs Urban)", .) %>%
  gsub("Age", "Age (years)", .) %>%
  gsub("Education", "Education level", .) %>%
  gsub("EmploymentUnemployed", "Unemployed (vs full-time)", .) %>%
  gsub("EmploymentEmployed part-time", "Part-time employed (vs full-time)", .) %>%
  gsub("Income_Insecurity", "Income insecurity", .) %>%
  gsub("Asset_Ownership", "Asset ownership", .) %>%
  gsub("Electricity_accessHas electricity", "Has electricity (vs no access)", .) %>%
  gsub("GDP_PPP_GroupGDP PPP \\(< \\$1,000\\)", "GDP < $1,000", .) %>%
  gsub("GDP_PPP_GroupGDP PPP \\(\\$1,000–\\$1,999\\)", "GDP $1,000–$1,999", .) %>%
  gsub("GDP_PPP_GroupGDP PPP \\(\\$2,000–\\$2,999\\)", "GDP $2,000–$2,999", .) %>%
  gsub("GDP_PPP_GroupGDP PPP \\(\\$3,000–\\$3,999\\)", "GDP $3,000–$3,999", .)



results_table <- data.frame(
  Variable = clean_var_names,
  Coefficient = round(coefs_predictors[, "Value"], 3),
  OR = round(OR, 3),
  CI_lower = round(ci_OR[, 1], 3),
  CI_upper = round(ci_OR[, 2], 3)
)


# 
# 9. Cutpoints
#
cutpoints <- coefs[grepl("\\|", rownames(coefs)), , drop = FALSE]
cutpoints_table <- data.frame(
  Threshold = rownames(cutpoints),
  Estimate = round(cutpoints[, "Value"], 3)
)

#
# 5️ Model Stats (with BIC added, no logLik)
# 
AIC_val <- AIC(model_ordinal)
BIC_val <- BIC(model_ordinal)
n_obs <- nobs(model_ordinal)
df_model <- attr(logLik(model_ordinal), "df")
logLik.val <- logLik(model_ordinal)
# Null model for pseudo R²
model_null <- update(model_ordinal, . ~ 1)

pseudo_r2_mcfadden <- 1 - (as.numeric(logLik(model_ordinal)) / as.numeric(logLik(model_null)))
pseudo_r2_coxsnell <- 1 - exp((2 / n_obs) * (as.numeric(logLik(model_null)) - as.numeric(logLik(model_ordinal))))
pseudo_r2_nagelkerke <- pseudo_r2_coxsnell / (1 - exp(2 * as.numeric(logLik(model_null)) / n_obs))

model_stats_line <- sprintf(
  "\\textit{Model statistics:} AIC = %.2f; BIC = %.2f; logLik = %.2f; N = %d; df = %d; \\\\ Pseudo R$^2$ (McFadden / Cox-Snell / Nagelkerke) = %.3f / %.3f / %.3f",
  AIC_val, BIC_val, logLik.val, n_obs, df_model,
  pseudo_r2_mcfadden, pseudo_r2_coxsnell, pseudo_r2_nagelkerke
)




# 
# 6. Create LaTeX Table for Model Results
# 
table_tex_main <- results_table %>%
  dplyr::select(Variable, Coefficient, OR, CI_lower, CI_upper) %>%
  kbl(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    align = "lcccc",
    caption = "Table X. Ordinal logistic regression (POLR) results for determinants of food insecurity in Sub-Saharan Africa (n = 49,137)",
    escape = FALSE
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "hold_position", "scale_down")) %>%
  footnote(
    general = model_stats_line,
    general_title = "",
    escape = FALSE
  )

#
# 7️. Create Cutpoints Table
# 
cutpoints_tex <- cutpoints_table %>%
  kbl(
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    caption = "Cutpoints (Thresholds) for Food Insecurity Categories",
    align = "lc",
    escape = FALSE
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position"))

# 
# 8️. Save Both Tables to a .tex File
# 
writeLines(
  c(as.character(table_tex_main), "\n\n", as.character(cutpoints_tex)),
  "Tab_Ord_Mod.tex"
)

results_table
model_stats_line
```
